# `create-noy-db` — guided scaffolder

Part of #EPIC (v0.3 release).

## Scope

Ship a brand-new standalone package `create-noy-db` (published to npm so that `npm create noy-db@latest`, `pnpm create noy-db`, `yarn create noy-db`, and `bun create noy-db` all work). Lives at `packages/create-noy-db/`. Built on `@clack/prompts`. Detects framework, package manager, and TypeScript usage from the target directory; otherwise generates a fresh template. Asks at most 8 questions, installs only the packages chosen, generates working code (not stubs), and runs an end-to-end integrity check before declaring success.

## Why

This is the front door of the v0.3 adoption story. A greenfield Vue/Nuxt developer must go from `npm create noy-db` to a running encrypted Pinia store in under two minutes. Without this, the v0.3 goal is impossible.

## Technical design

- New package `packages/create-noy-db/` with `bin: { "create-noy-db": "./dist/cli.js" }`.
- Prompts via `@clack/prompts`. AST-edits to existing `nuxt.config.ts` via `magicast`. Diff display via `diff` package; user must confirm before any file mutation.
- Question set (max 8): adapter, sync target, auth mode, multi-user yes/no, schema validator (zod | valibot | none), sample data yes/no, privacy guard hook yes/no, language (en | th).
- Templates live inside the package under `packages/create-noy-db/templates/` so the wizard works offline. One template per (framework × language) combination.
- Detects `package.json#packageManager` and lockfiles to choose the install command.
- Final step runs an integrity check: open compartment → write a record → read it → decrypt → verify ledger. Fails loudly if anything is wrong.
- Re-runnable inside an existing project to add collections (delegates to the same prompts as `nuxi noydb add`).
- Never reads or writes secrets; never asks for AWS credentials; never uploads telemetry. See ROADMAP.md "1. `create-noy-db`".

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] **Implementation:** `packages/create-noy-db/` builds with tsup, ESM-only, Node 20+, ships templates folder.
- [ ] **Unit tests:** `__tests__/` with at least 15 `it()` blocks covering: prompt flow happy path, each adapter branch, each package-manager detection, TypeScript vs JS detection, framework detection (Nuxt 4 / Vite / vanilla), magicast `nuxt.config.ts` patching, diff confirmation rejection aborts, secrets never written to disk, Thai prompt strings present, re-run mode in existing project, privacy-guard hook opt-in, integrity check failure aborts with non-zero exit, template files render without unresolved placeholders.
- [ ] **Integration tests:** at least one test that runs the compiled CLI in a temp dir against `@noy-db/memory` and verifies the integrity check passes.
- [ ] **E2E tests:** dedicated CI job that runs `npm create noy-db@latest` end-to-end in a clean temp dir for each (framework × adapter × pm) combination from the matrix, then runs `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm build` on the generated project. Matrix runs on macOS, Linux, Windows, Node 20 + 22.
- [ ] **Type tests:** prompt option types exported and asserted with `vitest expect-type`.
- [ ] **Docs:** `packages/create-noy-db/README.md` with the four package-manager invocations and the question list. Update `docs/getting-started.md` to lead with the wizard.
- [ ] **Changeset:** new package release at `0.3.0`.
- [ ] **CI:** new workflow job `e2e-scaffolder` with the matrix above; runs only on PRs touching `packages/create-noy-db/**` and on the release branch.
- [ ] **Bundle:** CLI bundle <100 KB gzipped (templates excluded).

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: #4 (defineNoydbStore — the templates emit code that uses it)
- Blocks: #10 (the demo can be generated by the scaffolder), #12 (release)
